2605.06510.title: Is One Layer Enough? Understanding Inference Dynamics in Tabular Foundation Models
2605.06510.paper_summary: Transformer-based tabular foundation models (TFMs) dominate small to medium tabular predictive benchmark tasks, yet their inference mechanisms remain largely unexplored. We present the first large-scale mechanistic study of layerwise dynamics in 6 state-of-the-art tabular in-context learning models. We explore how predictions emerge across depth, identify distinct stages of inference and reveal latent-space dynamics that differ from those of language models. Our findings indicate substantial depthwise redundancy across multiple models, suggesting iterative refinement with overlapping computations during inference stages. Guided by these insights, we design a proof-of-concept, looped single-layer model that uses only 20% of the original model's parameters while achieving comparable performance. The code is available at https://github.com/amirbalef/is_one_layer_enough.
2605.06510.paper_summary_zh: 基於Transformer的表格基礎模型（TFMs）主導了小型到中型的表格預測基準任務，但它們的推理機制仍然在很大程度上未被探索。我們展示了對六個最先進的表格上下文學習模型進行的首次大規模機制研究，研究層級動態。我們探討了預測如何在深度上出現，識別推理的不同階段，並揭示與語言模型不同的潛在空間動態。我們的研究結果顯示，跨多個模型存在顯著的深度冗餘，這表明在推理階段存在重疊計算的迭代精煉。根據這些見解，我們設計了一個概念驗證的循環單層模型，僅使用原始模型參數的20%，卻達到了可比的性能。代碼可在 https://github.com/amirbalef/is_one_layer_enough 獲得。
2605.06510.author: Amir Rezaei Balef et.al.
2605.06510.authors: Amir Rezaei Balef, Mykhailo Koshil, Katharina Eggensperger
